name: Bhai Mohinder Singh Thumri (Amritsar)
body: Bhai Mohinder Singh Thumri was born in 1934 into the home of Sardar Sunder Singh, who owned a harmonium repair shop in Hall Bazaar, Amritsar. The shop was called ‘Golden Harmonium Workers’. Bhai Mohinder Singh Ji had an interest in music since young, therefore his father sent him to Bhagat Singh Sangra for lessons. Next, he became a student of film music director, Pandit Hussan Lal, who was a student of the great Pandit Dalip Chandar Vedi. Finally, he came under the tutelage of Pandit Dalip Chandar Vedi himself, who taught him the fine nuances of classical music. He also had the blessings of Ustad Barkat Ali Khan of the Patiala Gharana of music. In 1956, he emerged as the winner of the annual competition of ‘thumri & dadra’ organised by All India Radio. Prior to that, he was also given the title of ‘Surmani’ by Lata Mangeshkar in a competition in Bombay. If anyone has more recordings Bhai Mohinder Singh Thumri to share, please contact us.
